body.title: Capabilities ship two-sided and get consumed deliberately
body.statement: The protocol has a producer side and a consumer side. PRODUCER (two-sided-build): a ticket that adds a new capability ships the handler AND the skill/workflow consumer together — never just one half. CONSUMER (workflow side): before claiming work, scan the ticket's tags and Affected files against the live capability surface (MCP tool names, datacenter manifest); if a minion or rack device would do the work better than inline, surface the delegate option before the claim — the prompt is mandatory, the delegation is Akien's call. POST-SPRINT: ask 'what from this sprint would I deposit into the palace?' — default skip; deposit only non-obvious reasoning.

two-sided-build.check: Capability tickets (tag 'Capability', or description creating an MCP capability / shim / handler) must list both a HANDLER path (unseen_university/devices/, devlab/claudecode/mcp_*, **/device*.py, **/capability*.py) and a SKILL CONSUMER path (skills/) in Affected files.
two-sided-build.exemption: A description line 'exempt from unseenuniversity/rules/capability-protocol/two-sided-build' with a stated reason (pure consumer of an existing surface, or the enforcer ticket itself). The exemption is noted in audit output.

body.why: A capability without a consumer is dead weight nobody exercises, and a consumer without a handler is a hallucinated surface — both are the two-sided cost of specifying intent showing up late instead of at filing (CP4: the friction belongs up-front where it's visible).
